skills.name: Web Research
skills.description: Deep web research with structured markdown output
skills.content:
# Web Research

When performing research:
1. Search the web for the most current information
2. Cross-reference multiple sources for accuracy
3. Focus on practical, actionable findings
4. Save findings to the research/ directory as markdown

## Output Format
- Executive Summary (3-5 sentences)
- Key Findings (bulleted list)
- Opportunities / Risks
- Recommended Next Steps
- Sources (links and references)

skills.name: Eisenhower Matrix Triage
skills.description: Applies Eisenhower matrix logic to prioritize work
skills.content:
# Eisenhower Matrix Triage

## Quadrant Definitions
| Quadrant | Criteria | Action |
|----------|----------|--------|
| DO | important + urgent | Work on immediately |
| SCHEDULE | important + not-urgent | Block time |
| DELEGATE | not-important + urgent | Assign to agent |
| ELIMINATE | not-important + not-urgent | Drop or defer |

## Triage Rules
1. New tasks default to SCHEDULE unless deadline < 48 hours
2. DELEGATE tasks should always have assignedTo set
3. Review DO quadrant daily

skills.name: Task Management
skills.description: Manages tasks in Mission Control via JSON data files
skills.content:
# Task Management

All data lives in mission-control/data/ as JSON files.

## Quick Reference
- AI Context: mission-control/data/ai-context.md (read FIRST)
- Tasks: mission-control/data/tasks.json
- Goals: mission-control/data/goals.json
- Projects: mission-control/data/projects.json

## Creating a Task
Required: id, title, description, importance, urgency, kanban, assignedTo
Generate IDs as: task_{Date.now()}

## After Any Data Modification
Run pnpm gen:context in mission-control/ to regenerate ai-context.md

skills.name: Visual Explainer
skills.description: Generate rich HTML pages and slide decks for diagrams, reviews, plans, data tables, and project recaps. From nicobailon/visual-explainer (MIT, 4.2K stars).
skills.content:
# Visual Explainer

Generate self-contained HTML files for technical diagrams, visualizations, and data tables. Save all output to mission-control/data/deliverables/. Never fall back to ASCII art when producing deliverables.

**Source:** https://github.com/nicobailon/visual-explainer

## Proactive Table Rendering
When you are about to present tabular data (comparisons, audits, feature matrices, status reports), generate an HTML page instead of ASCII tables. Threshold: 4+ rows or 3+ columns → render as HTML automatically.

## Commands
| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| /generate-web-diagram | HTML diagram for any topic |
| /generate-visual-plan | Visual implementation plan |
| /generate-slides | Magazine-quality slide deck |
| /diff-review | Visual diff review with architecture comparison |
| /plan-review | Compare plan against codebase with risk assessment |
| /project-recap | Mental model snapshot for context-switching |
| /fact-check | Verify document accuracy against actual code |

## Workflow
1. **Think** — Pick an aesthetic direction. Vary each time. Forbidden: neon dashboard (cyan+magenta+purple), gradient mesh blobs, Inter+violet generics.
   - Constrained aesthetics (prefer): Blueprint, Editorial, Paper/ink, Monochrome terminal
   - Flexible aesthetics (use carefully): IDE-inspired (Dracula, Nord, Catppuccin, Solarized, Gruvbox, Rosé Pine)
2. **Structure** — Choose rendering approach by content type:
   - Architecture (text-heavy): CSS Grid cards + flow arrows
   - Architecture (topology): Mermaid diagrams
   - Flowchart/pipeline/sequence/ER/state machine/mind map: Mermaid
   - Data tables: HTML `<table>` with semantic markup
   - Timeline: CSS central line + cards
   - Dashboard: CSS Grid + Chart.js
3. **Style** — Typography is the diagram. Pick distinctive font pairings. Use CSS custom properties for theming. Support both light and dark modes via prefers-color-scheme.
4. **Output** — Self-contained HTML file. All CSS inline, all JS inline. CDN links for Mermaid/Chart.js only. Save to deliverables/ with descriptive filename.

## Mermaid Best Practices
- Always use `theme: 'base'` with custom `themeVariables` matching page palette
- Use `layout: 'elk'` for complex graphs (10+ nodes)
- Prefer `flowchart TD` over `flowchart LR` for complex diagrams
- Center with flex, add zoom controls (+/−/reset) to every container
- Never define `.node` as a page-level CSS class (collides with Mermaid internals)

## Key Principle
Every deliverable should be visually rich. Even essays, blog posts, and articles get visual treatment — extract structure into cards, diagrams, grids, tables. The swap test: if you replaced your styling with a generic dark theme and nobody noticed, you haven't designed anything.
